basic_details.job_title: HPPSC Assistant Professor Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 369 Posts
basic_details.department: Department of Higher Education, Himachal Pradesh
basic_details.short_information: The Himachal Pradesh Public Service Commission has opened 369 Assistant Professor (College Cadre) vacancies across 22 separate subject-wise notifications (Advertisement No. 34/6-2026 to 55/6-2026), released on 17 June 2026. Each subject has its own application on the HPPSC OTR portal, closing on 14 July 2026 (11:59 PM), with selected candidates engaged as Job Trainees at Rs. 35,000/- per month.

application_fee.details: The application fee is a flat Rs. 600 for all candidates, paid online via Debit Card, Credit Card, UPI, or other digital modes, and is non-refundable and non-transferable once paid. Since each subject is a separate application, the fee must be paid afresh for every subject applied to. | Once submitted, no corrections or modifications are allowed, so candidates should verify every field before final submission. | Applications with missing or poor-quality scans are liable to rejection, so candidates should keep the following ready: Matriculation certificate for age proof, degree certificates with all years' marksheets, experience certificate (wherever required), and valid category certificates (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/Ex-Servicemen/WFF/PwBD) in the HP Government's prescribed format. Reserved category candidates additionally need a Bonafide Himachali certificate, and EWS applicants need an Income & Asset Certificate or a BDO-countersigned BPL Certificate along with a non-SC/ST/OBC certificate.

qualification.details: HPPSC requires a Master's degree with at least 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/OBC and PwBD) in the relevant subject, a qualifying NET/SLET/SET score, and an age between 18 and 45 years as on 1 January 2026. The subject of the Master's degree must correspond to the post applied for. | Master's degree with 55% marks (or equivalent grade) in the relevant subject from a recognized university, or an equivalent recognized foreign degree. 5% relaxation (55% to 50%) for SC/ST/OBC (non-creamy layer) and Physically/Visually Handicapped candidates. 5% relaxation for Ph.D. holders who completed their Master's before September 1991. NET (UGC/CSIR) or equivalent SLET/SET is the minimum eligibility condition for the post. Ph.D. holders are exempt from NET/SLET/SET, provided the Ph.D. was awarded in regular mode, evaluated by at least two external examiners, included an open viva-voce, and is backed by at least two published papers (one refereed) plus two conference presentations. Candidates who qualified SET-2026 are also eligible; the window will reopen for them if the SET-2026 result comes after the regular closing date. | The age is calculated as on 1 January 2026, based on the Matriculation certificate. A 5-year relaxation in upper age applies only to Bonafide SC/ST/OBC, Ward of Freedom Fighters, and Persons with Disabilities of HP, and only if the post is reserved for that category in the subject applied for; HP Government employees and Ex-Servicemen get relaxation per separate government instructions.

salary_details.details: Selected candidates are engaged as Job Trainees at a fixed Rs. 35,000/- per month, uniform across all 22 subjects, under the Department of Personnel's notification No. Per(AP)-C-B(15)-3/2024 dated 19 July 2025, which governs the job-trainee engagement scheme across HP Government departments.

selection_process: 1 Application Scrutiny Eligible candidates provisionally admitted; rejected candidates get 10 days to represent
2 Paper-I: Screening Test 1-hour MCQ, 100 marks (HP GK-30, National/International Affairs-30, Hindi-20, English-20); qualifying only
3 Paper-II: Subject Aptitude Test (SAT) 3-hour descriptive test, 120 marks, subject-specific syllabus; held same day as Paper-I
4 Document Verification Shortlisting per category-wise zone ratio (1:5 for 11+ posts; 1:5+3 for up to 10 posts)
5 Personality Test 30 marks, at HPPSC office Shimla only; minimum pass 40% General / 30% Reserved
6 Final Merit SAT (120) + Personality Test (30) = 150 marks total
Selection runs through a two-paper written test on the same day
with final merit based on Paper-II plus interview marks. The process starts with document scrutiny and ends with a 30-mark interview at HPPSC's Shimla office.
Paper-I is qualifying only and does not count toward merit; candidates scoring zero or negative marks are excluded outright. There is 0.25 mark negative marking per wrong
or blank answer in objective tests
and the SAT qualifying mark is 45% General / 40% Reserved.

rows.details: Department of Higher Education, Himachal Pradesh

content_sections.heading: HPPSC Assistant Professor 2026 - Subject-Wise Vacancies (369 Posts)
content_sections.text: The 369 posts are spread unevenly across 22 subjects, with English (39 posts) carrying the largest share, followed by Chemistry and Hindi (34 posts each) , while Dance and Tabla have only 2 posts each. All posts, regardless of subject, carry the same Rs. 35,000/- monthly pay. Every subject's vacancies are further split into UR, SC, ST, OBC, and EWS, with sub-quotas for Ex-Servicemen, Ward of Freedom Fighters, and Persons with Benchmark Disabilities wherever applicable. For instance, the English post (39 vacancies) is divided as UR=16, SC=05, ST=03, OBC=02, EWS=04, with the rest under sub-categories, while smaller posts like Dance and Tabla are entirely unreserved. Candidates should verify the category matrix in their subject's specific notification PDF, as it directly affects age relaxation and cut-off eligibility.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: HPPSC requires a Master's degree with at least 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/OBC and PwBD) in the relevant subject , a qualifying NET/SLET/SET score, and an age between 18 and 45 years as on 1 January 2026 . The subject of the Master's degree must correspond to the post applied for. Master's degree with 55% marks (or equivalent grade) in the relevant subject from a recognized university, or an equivalent recognized foreign degree. 5% relaxation (55% to 50%) for SC/ST/OBC (non-creamy layer) and Physically/Visually Handicapped candidates. 5% relaxation for Ph.D. holders who completed their Master's before September 1991. NET (UGC/CSIR) or equivalent SLET/SET is the minimum eligibility condition for the post. Ph.D. holders are exempt from NET/SLET/SET , provided the Ph.D. was awarded in regular mode, evaluated by at least two external examiners, included an open viva-voce, and is backed by at least two published papers (one refereed) plus two conference presentations. Candidates who qualified SET-2026 are also eligible; the window will reopen for them if the SET-2026 result comes after the regular closing date. The age is calculated as on 1 January 2026 , based on the Matriculation certificate. A 5-year relaxation in upper age applies only to Bonafide SC/ST/OBC, Ward of Freedom Fighters, and Persons with Disabilities of HP, and only if the post is reserved for that category in the subject applied for ; HP Government employees and Ex-Servicemen get relaxation per separate government instructions.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee and How to Apply
content_sections.text: The application fee is a flat Rs. 600 for all candidates, paid online via Debit Card, Credit Card, UPI, or other digital modes, and is non-refundable and non-transferable once paid. Since each subject is a separate application, the fee must be paid afresh for every subject applied to. Once submitted, no corrections or modifications are allowed , so candidates should verify every field before final submission. Applications with missing or poor-quality scans are liable to rejection, so candidates should keep the following ready: Matriculation certificate for age proof, degree certificates with all years' marksheets , experience certificate (wherever required), and valid category certificates (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/Ex-Servicemen/WFF/PwBD) in the HP Government's prescribed format. Reserved category candidates additionally need a Bonafide Himachali certificate , and EWS applicants need an Income & Asset Certificate or a BDO-countersigned BPL Certificate along with a non-SC/ST/OBC certificate.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection runs through a two-paper written test on the same day, followed by a Personality Test , with final merit based on Paper-II plus interview marks. The process starts with document scrutiny and ends with a 30-mark interview at HPPSC's Shimla office. Paper-I is qualifying only and does not count toward merit; candidates scoring zero or negative marks are excluded outright. There is 0.25 mark negative marking per wrong, multiple, or blank answer in objective tests, and the SAT qualifying mark is 45% General / 40% Reserved .

rows.details: Eligible candidates provisionally admitted; rejected candidates get 10 days to represent
1-hour MCQ, 100 marks (HP GK-30, National/International Affairs-30, Hindi-20, English-20); qualifying only
3-hour descriptive test, 120 marks, subject-specific syllabus; held same day as Paper-I
Shortlisting per category-wise zone ratio (1:5 for 11+ posts; 1:5+3 for up to 10 posts)
30 marks, at HPPSC office Shimla only; minimum pass 40% General / 30% Reserved

content_sections.heading: Salary
content_sections.text: Selected candidates are engaged as Job Trainees at a fixed Rs. 35,000/- per month , uniform across all 22 subjects, under the Department of Personnel's notification No. Per(AP)-C-B(15)-3/2024 dated 19 July 2025, which governs the job-trainee engagement scheme across HP Government departments.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: With 22 separate codes (34/6-2026 to 55/6-2026), an incorrect subject selection cannot be corrected after submission. Uploading unclear documents: Illegible scanned certificates lead to rejection, so scan at adequate resolution. Submitting without a final review: No corrections are allowed post-submission, so date of birth and category should be double-checked beforehand. Skipping NET/SLET/SET proof upload: Missing this can cause provisional rejection even for otherwise eligible candidates. Missing the answer key objection window: Objections to the Paper-I key must be raised within 5 days of publication with Rs. 100 per question; this window cannot be reopened. Assuming blanket relaxation: Age and qualification relaxations apply only if the post is reserved for that category in the specific subject.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for HPPSC Assistant Professor 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application and pay the fee is 14 July 2026, 11:59 PM, applicable to all 22 subject advertisements.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in HPPSC Assistant Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 369 total vacancies spread across 22 subjects, ranging from 39 posts in English to 2 posts each in Dance and Tabla.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application fee for HPPSC Assistant Professor 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is a flat Rs. 600, charged uniformly to all candidates irrespective of category or gender, and it is non-refundable once paid.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for HPPSC Assistant Professor 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 45 years of age as on 1 January 2026, with a 5-year relaxation available for SC/ST/OBC, WFF, and PwBD candidates of Himachal Pradesh where the post is reserved for that category.

faq.question: Q5. What educational qualification is required for HPPSC Assistant Professor posts?
faq.answer: Candidates need a Master's degree with at least 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD) in the relevant subject, along with a qualifying NET/SLET/SET score; Ph.D. holders meeting specific conditions are exempt from NET/SLET/SET.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for HPPSC Assistant Professor 2026?
faq.answer: Selection involves a qualifying Paper-I Screening Test (100 marks), a Paper-II Subject Aptitude Test (120 marks), and a Personality Test (30 marks), with final merit based on Paper-II plus Personality Test marks (150 marks total).

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary of HPPSC Assistant Professor (Job Trainee)?
faq.answer: Selected candidates receive a fixed Job Trainee emolument of Rs. 35,000 per month, applicable across all 22 subjects.

faq.question: Q8. How can I apply for HPPSC Assistant Professor 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online through the OTR portal on www.hppsc.hp.gov.in/hppsc, selecting the specific subject advertisement, uploading required documents, and paying the Rs. 600 fee before the closing date.

faq.question: Q9. Can SET-2026 qualified candidates apply for these posts?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates who qualify SET-2026 are also eligible to apply, and the advertisement window will be reopened for them if the SET-2026 result is declared after the regular closing date.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website for HPPSC Assistant Professor 2026?
faq.answer: The official website is http://www.hppsc.hp.gov.in/hppsc, where candidates can find subject-wise notifications, apply online, and download e-admit cards.
